title: Percentage to Double Conversion
prompt_md:
## Goal

Create a `Percentage` class that stores an integer percent value and provides an explicit conversion to `double` that returns the decimal form (e.g., 75 becomes 0.75).

## Input

- A single line containing one integer `P` (0 <= P <= 100).

## Output

- A single line containing the decimal equivalent printed with exactly 2 decimal places, followed by a newline.

## Requirements

- Define a class `Percentage` with a constructor that takes an `int`.
- Overload `operator double()` as an `explicit` conversion operator.
- In `main`, read the input, construct a `Percentage`, explicitly cast it to `double`, and print the result using `std::fixed` and `std::setprecision(2)`.
- Do not use any global variables.

title: Boolean Validity Check
prompt_md:
## Goal

Create an `ID` class that stores an integer identifier and converts to `bool` -- an `ID` is valid (true) when its value is positive, and invalid (false) when it is zero or negative. Use the explicit `operator bool()` special rule to test objects directly in `if` statements.

## Input

- Line 1: a single integer `N` (1 <= N <= 50).
- Line 2: `N` space-separated integers (-1000 <= each <= 1000).

## Output

- For each integer, print `valid` if the ID is valid or `invalid` otherwise, one per line.

## Requirements

- Define a class `ID` with a constructor that takes an `int`.
- Overload `explicit operator bool()` that returns `true` when the stored value is greater than 0.
- In the loop, construct an `ID` from each input value and use it directly in an `if` statement (do not cast or call any other method).
- Do not use any global variables.
